summary: Shorten `SYMBOL_ID` to reduce agent IO churn: switch from `sym:v1:<hex_sha256>` to `sym:v2:<hex_sha256_96>` (24 hex chars) while preserving deterministic derivation from the canonical symbol key.
decisions: Use a fixed 96-bit SHA-256 prefix (24 lowercase hex chars) to keep IDs short but collision-resistant for repo-scale symbol counts.
Treat this as a clean breaking change: update validators/specs and regenerate all `kb/gen/*` artifacts in downstream repos.
pitfalls: Any change to ID format rewrites `kb/gen/symbols.jsonl` and `tree.jsonl` `top_symbols`; regenerate artifacts after formatting/EOF fixers converge to avoid pre-commit ping-pong.
Update all typed-surface examples in specs/dev plans so agents don’t copy stale `sym:v1:` shapes.
